title: Puppy Scratching Ears and Shaking Head: Causes and Care Guide
meta_desc: Is your puppy shaking their head? Learn about ear mites, infections, and when to see a vet in our empathetic veterinary triage guide.
content_html:
Understanding Ear Discomfort in Puppies

It can be very distressing to see your new puppy constantly pawing at their ears or shaking their head vigorously. These behaviors are primary indicators that your puppy is experiencing irritation, itchiness, or pain within the ear canal. In young dogs, these symptoms are most frequently associated with ear mites, yeast overgrowth, or bacterial infections. Because puppies are still developing, their ears can be sensitive environments where moisture and debris easily become trapped.

Is It an Emergency?

Urgency Level: Low. While the constant scratching is uncomfortable for your puppy, it is rarely a life-threatening emergency. However, it does require a veterinary appointment within the next 24 to 48 hours. If left untreated, the underlying cause can lead to a secondary infection or a painful aural hematoma—a blood-filled swelling of the ear flap caused by the trauma of excessive shaking.

How Photos Can Assist With Triage

Providing a clear, well-lit photo of the inner ear flap and the opening of the ear canal can significantly help a veterinary professional assess the situation. A photo allows us to see the color and consistency of any discharge; for example, dark debris resembling coffee grounds often suggests ear mites, while yellow or green discharge may indicate a bacterial infection. Seeing the level of redness and swelling helps us determine how quickly your pet needs to be seen.

faqs.question: Can I use human ear drops on my puppy?
faqs.answer: No, you should never use human medications or over-the-counter cleaners without a vet's approval, as some ingredients can be toxic to dogs or damaging if the eardrum is ruptured.

faqs.question: Are ear mites common in puppies?
faqs.answer: Yes, ear mites are very common in puppies and are highly contagious between pets. If you have other animals, they may also need to be checked.

faqs.question: What happens if I ignore the head shaking?
faqs.answer: Ignoring the symptoms can lead to chronic inflammation, permanent scarring of the ear canal, or a ruptured eardrum, which is much more difficult and expensive to treat.
